summary: Scorching Passenger, Brent debut on Cold Rain, solid Althea
blurb: The show delivers a fiery Passenger and a memorable first‑time Brent Mydland appearance on Cold Rain And Snow. Highlights also include a tear‑jerking Peggy-O, rolling New Minglewood Blues, and an uplifting Althea. Overall the performance is strong with occasional guitar wobble but plenty of high‑energy moments.
review: November 10, 1979 at Crisler Arena captures the Dead in a transitional yet exciting phase. The first set kicks off with Brent Mydland’s debut on Cold Rain And Snow, where his organ cuts loose and his vocal phrasing adds fresh color. A rolling New Minglewood Blues builds momentum into a heartfelt Peggy‑O that many listeners describe as tear‑jerking. Mid‑set the band unleashes a scorching Passenger, showcasing Garcia’s occasional technical hiccups but also moments of pure fire. The second set stretches out with a 40‑minute post‑space jam, delivering a bright Althea and an adventurous Terrapin Station segue into Playing In The Band, while the classic Not Fade Away → Black Peter → Sugar Magnolia finale rounds out a solid, if not flawless, night. Brent’s synth‑filled Looks Like Rain and swirling organ on Cold Rain further underscore his immediate impact, and Bob Weir steps up whenever Garcia’s guitar wavers, keeping the rhythm tight. Despite some pitch issues in the available soundboard source, the show remains a rewarding listen for fans seeking both historic milestones and vibrant improvisation.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four out of five recordings are marked positive, highlighting strong moments (Passenger, Cold Rain And Snow debut, Althea) with only one mixed review noting occasional guitar issues. Consistent praise across multiple sources warrants a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Most complete soundboard source with full set, despite minor pitch issues; offers the clearest representation of the entire performance.

key_highlights: Scorching Passenger that puts the band on fire.
Brent Mydland’s debut on Cold Rain And Snow with cutting‑loose organ and vocals.
Emotionally resonant Peggy-O and rolling New Minglewood Blues.
Lively Althea performance captured in a crisp soundboard mix.

band_performance.Jerry: Shows occasional technical guitar wobble but delivers strong vocal phrasing and solid form on Cold Rain, High Time, and an incendiary Passenger.
band_performance.Bob: Steps up when Garcia falters, providing steady rhythm guitar and reliable vocals throughout the night.
band_performance.Brent: Makes a standout debut, cutting loose on Passenger, swirling organ on Cold Rain, synth‑filled Looks Like Rain, and delivering emotive vocal lines that lift the show.
